Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "revenue" or "March" or "comp" or "Victor" (or similar) to find Victor Osei's email to Hana Kim about the March P&L shortfall. Expected findings: Victor flags that broker comp revenue is about $3,500 below projection on three files -- the Allen file through Pennymac, the Cook file through Caliber, and the Flores file through AmeriHome. He asks Hana to pull lender comp statements and figure out where the gap is. Robert wants clean numbers before the partner review next Thursday.
OE 2: Search for loans using mortgage_los_search_loans with queries related to "Allen" or "Daniel Allen" (or similar) to find loan LN-2026-00210. Expected findings: $435,000 conventional loan through Pennymac Wholesale (lender_id los_lender_c3f3bd7cfc93), assigned to Derek Moss (los_staff_f9aa4c3c2fcb), closed 2026-03-17, rate locked 2026-01-20, property at 1315 Freedom Dr, Charlotte, NC 28269.
OE 3: Search for loans using mortgage_los_search_loans with queries related to "Cook" or "Stephanie Cook" (or similar) to find loan LN-2026-00199. Expected findings: $382,000 conventional loan through Caliber Home Loans (lender_id los_lender_723b5e99cb66), assigned to Keisha Williams (los_staff_9f0f19845970), closed 2026-03-25, rate locked 2026-01-29, property at 7973 Harrisburg Cabarrus Rd, Charlotte, NC 28217.
OE 4: Search for loans using mortgage_los_search_loans with queries related to "Flores" or "Christopher Flores" (or similar) to find loan LN-2026-00471. Expected findings: $264,000 conventional loan through AmeriHome Mortgage (lender_id los_lender_533a73fed6b6), assigned to Priya Desai (los_staff_930d770d3309), closed 2026-03-14, rate locked 2026-01-18, property at 373 Monroe Rd, Charlotte, NC 28204.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Allen" or "KMP-2026-00210" (or similar) to find the broker comp invoice for LN-2026-00210. Expected findings: Invoice INV-scenario-00210, DocNumber KMP-2026-00210, TotalAmt $5,437.50, booked at 1.25% on $435,000. Balance $5,437.50 (full amount outstanding, meaning lender payment has not been applied).
OE 6: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Cook" or "KMP-2026-00199" (or similar) to find the broker comp invoice for LN-2026-00199. Expected findings: Invoice INV-scenario-00199, DocNumber KMP-2026-00199, TotalAmt $4,393.00, booked at 1.15% on $382,000. Balance $4,393.00.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Flores" or "KMP-2026-00471" (or similar) to find the broker comp invoice for LN-2026-00471. Expected findings: Invoice INV-scenario-00471, DocNumber KMP-2026-00471, TotalAmt $2,904.00, booked at 1.1% on $264,000. Balance $1,254.00 (partial -- $1,650 received in operating, $1,254 missing).
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Pennymac" or "comp schedule" or "conventional comp" or "Cheryl Watkins" (or similar) to find the Pennymac comp rate change notification. Expected findings: Email from cheryl.watkins@pennymac.com to camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com explaining that Pennymac reduced conventional broker comp from 1.25% to 0.875% for all loans locked on or after January 15. This is the root cause of the Allen/Pennymac shortfall -- the loan locked January 20 (after the cutoff), so actual comp is 0.875% x $435,000 = $3,806.25, creating a $1,631.25 gap vs the $5,437.50 Hana booked.
OE 9: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Pennymac" or "comp" or "87.5 bps" (or similar) to find Camille Foster's rate sheet announcement in #rate-watch. Expected findings: Camille's March 2 message in C005 (#rate-watch) mentioning that Pennymac dropped conventional comp to 87.5 bps for locks after 1/15. This corroborates the email from Cheryl Watkins and confirms the comp change was communicated to LOs but not to finance.
OE 10: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find Grace Yamamoto's exception pricing approval for the Cook/Caliber file. Expected findings: CRM engagement crm_engagement_scenario_comp_01 titled "Exception pricing approval - Cook file" where Grace approved taking comp at 1.0% instead of standard 1.15% for Caliber conventional as a one-time exception. Grace did not loop in finance. This is the root cause of the Cook/Caliber shortfall -- actual comp is 1.0% x $382,000 = $3,820.00, creating a $573.00 gap vs the $4,393.00 Hana booked.
OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "AmeriHome" or "split payment" or "Megan Holt" or "Flores" or "escrow" (or similar) to find the AmeriHome split payment notification. Expected findings: Email from megan.holt@amerihome.com to camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com confirming that the $2,904.00 comp on the Flores file was split into two wires -- $1,650.00 to operating account and $1,254.00 to escrow trust account by mistake. This is the root cause of the Flores/AmeriHome shortfall -- the full comp was paid but $1,254 went to the wrong internal account.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "DeLuca" or "DL-4107" (or similar) to find the past-due DeLuca appraisal batch bill. Expected findings: Bill BILL-2026-0410-014, DocNumber DL-4107, TotalAmt $2,450.00, Balance $2,450.00 (unpaid), DueDate 2026-04-20 (past due), from DeLuca Appraisal Group. Line item description says "March appraisal invoices batch."
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DeLuca" or "DL-4107" or "March Invoice Batch" (or similar) to find the DeLuca batch invoice email sent to Hana. Expected findings: Email from billing@delucaappraisal.com to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com listing 5 line items totaling $2,450. Critically, one line item is for 9701 Selwyn Ave (LN-2026-00596) at $475 -- but this appraisal was actually performed by Carolina Valuation, not DeLuca.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "Carolina" or "CVS-8831" (or similar) to find the past-due Carolina Valuation appraisal batch bill. Expected findings: Bill BILL-2026-0410-015, DocNumber CVS-8831, TotalAmt $1,745.00, Balance $1,745.00 (unpaid), DueDate 2026-04-20 (past due), from Carolina Valuation Services. Line item description says "March appraisal invoices batch."
OE 15: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Carolina Valuation" or "CVS-8831" (or similar) to find the Carolina Valuation batch invoice email sent to Hana. Expected findings: Email from ap@carolinavaluation.com to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com listing 4 line items totaling $1,745. One line item is for 8625 Freedom Dr (LN-2026-00241) at $525 -- but this is a VA loan and the VA appraisal rate should be $475, not $525.
OE 16: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "appraisal" or "DeLuca" or "Carolina Valuation" or "vendor switch" or "LN-2026-00596" or "Selwyn" (or similar) to find Elena Marchetti's message about the vendor switch. Expected findings: Elena's April 8 message in C002 (#loan-processing) explaining she had to switch the appraisal on LN-2026-00596 (9701 Selwyn Ave) from DeLuca to Carolina Valuation mid-process because DeLuca could not meet the timeline.
OE 17: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "VA" or "$525" or "$475" or "LN-2026-00241" or "Freedom Dr" (or similar) to find Sofia Reyes's message about the VA appraisal overbilling. Expected findings: Sofia's April 13 message in C002 (#loan-processing) flagging that Carolina Valuation billed $525 for the Moss VA file (LN-2026-00241) but the VA appraisal rate should be $475 -- they billed the conventional rate by mistake.
OE 18: Search for the loan on LN-2026-00241 using mortgage_los_search_loans (or mortgage_los_get_loan) with query "LN-2026-00241" (or similar) to confirm the loan type. Expected findings: LN-2026-00241 is a VA loan ($421,000), assigned to Derek Moss, closed 2026-04-23, through Freedom Mortgage. The VA loan type confirms the appraisal should be billed at $475, not $525.
OE 19: Search Stripe transfers using stripe_list_transfers (or similar) to find commission transfers to Marcus Webb's departed account. Expected findings: 5 transfers totaling $7,655 to acct_scenario_644_departed (Marcus Webb Consulting), including tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA for $2,100 on LN-2026-00608 with metadata "auto_generated: true". The connected account metadata shows "departed: 2026-03-15".
OE 20: Search for loan LN-2026-00608 using mortgage_los_search_loans (or mortgage_los_get_loan) with query "LN-2026-00608" (or similar) to verify current LO assignment. Expected findings: LN-2026-00608 is a $231,000 conventional loan through Homepoint Financial, closed 2026-04-03, assigned to James Thornton (los_staff_ef0febeeff53) -- NOT Marcus Webb. This confirms the commission was routed to the wrong person.
OE 21: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Webb" or "commission" or "Thornton" (or similar) to find Hana's email to Grace about the Webb commission leak. Expected findings: Email thread between hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Hana flags the $2,100 transfer on LN-2026-00608 as the fifth transfer to Webb's account ($7,655 total). Grace replies that the file was reassigned on March 16, instructs Hana to loop in Raj, and says to hold all April commission payouts. Grace CCs raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 22: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Webb" or "pipeline reassignment" or "LN-2026-00608" or "Thornton" (or similar) to find Grace's pipeline reassignment announcement. Expected findings: Grace's March 16 message in C002 (#loan-processing) listing the reassignments: LN-2026-00608 to James Thornton, LN-2026-00525 to Amy Chen, LN-2026-00527 to Derek Moss.
OE 23: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find Grace's pipeline reassignment note. Expected findings: CRM engagement crm_engagement_scenario_comm_01 titled "Webb pipeline reassignment complete" confirming LOS assignments updated, and noting that Stripe commission routing and QuickBooks payee records still need to be updated. This confirms the Stripe routing was flagged as needing a fix but was never completed.
OE 24: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "close-out" or "AP" or "April" or "Victor" (or similar) to find Victor's April 27 follow-up email to Hana. Expected findings: Victor asks for status on the March comp reconciliation, notes the two past-due appraisal batch bills in AP, and mentions the Webb commission overpayment needs to be reversed or reclassified for the P&L. Robert is asking for numbers tomorrow (April 28).
OE 25: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (CC victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) summarizing the full reconciliation findings across all three scenarios: the lender comp shortfalls with root causes and correct amounts for each loan, the vendor billing errors on the two appraisal batch invoices, and the ongoing Webb commission routing issue with the total misdirected amount.
OE 26: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com asking about the Pennymac comp rate change and why finance was not notified, and requesting confirmation on the AmeriHome split payment so the $1,254 can be moved from escrow trust to operating.
OE 27: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com about the Stripe commission routing issue on LN-2026-00608 and requesting that all reassigned Webb loans have their Stripe customer metadata updated to reflect the current LO assignments.
OE 28: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in a Slack channel (C002 #loan-processing or similar) notifying the team about the vendor billing corrections needed -- specifically that the DeLuca batch bill includes a charge for LN-2026-00596 that should be billed by Carolina Valuation, and that the Carolina Valuation bill has a $525 charge on the VA file LN-2026-00241 that should be $475.
OE 29: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) documenting the April month-end reconciliation findings, including the three lender comp discrepancies with root causes and amounts, the two vendor billing errors, and the Webb commission routing issue with total exposure of $7,655.
OE 30: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 2 lookup (los_loan_cceffbf41de5 for LN-2026-00210), documenting the broker comp discrepancy -- Pennymac paid at 0.875% ($3,806.25) instead of the booked 1.25% ($5,437.50) due to the January 15 comp rate change, creating a $1,631.25 shortfall.
OE 31: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 3 lookup (los_loan_14035be9aa05 for LN-2026-00199), documenting the broker comp discrepancy -- Caliber paid at 1.0% ($3,820.00) instead of the booked 1.15% ($4,393.00) due to Grace's exception pricing approval, creating a $573.00 shortfall.
OE 32: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 4 lookup (los_loan_cb25a3f351f1 for LN-2026-00471), documenting the broker comp issue -- AmeriHome paid the correct $2,904.00 but $1,254.00 was misrouted to the escrow trust account instead of operating. Needs internal transfer to resolve.
OE 33: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 20 lookup for LN-2026-00608, documenting the commission routing error -- $2,100 commission was auto-transferred to Marcus Webb's departed account instead of James Thornton. Part of $7,655 total misdirected since Webb's March 15 departure.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Line item description says "March appraisal invoices batch."
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DeLuca" or "DL-4107" or "March Invoice Batch" (or similar) to find the DeLuca batch invoice email sent to Hana. Expected findings: Email from billing@delucaappraisal.com to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com listing 5 line items totaling $2,450. Critically, one line item is for 9701 Selwyn Ave (LN-2026-00596) at $475 -- but this appraisal was actually performed by Carolina Valuation, not DeLuca.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "Carolina" or "CVS-8831" (or similar) to find the past-due Carolina Valuation appraisal batch bill. Expected findings: Bill BILL-2026-0410-015, DocNumber CVS-8831, TotalAmt $1,745.00, Balance $1,745.00 (unpaid), DueDate 2026-04-20 (past due), from Carolina Valuation Services. Line item description says "March appraisal invoices batch."
OE 15: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Carolina Valuation" or "CVS-8831" (or similar) to find the Carolina Valuation batch invoice email sent to Hana. Expected findings: Email from ap@carolinavaluation.com to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com listing 4 line items totaling $1,745. One line item is for 8625 Freedom Dr (LN-2026-00241) at $525 -- but this is a VA loan and the VA appraisal rate should be $475, not $525.
OE 16: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "appraisal" or "DeLuca" or "Carolina Valuation" or "vendor switch" or "LN-2026-00596" or "Selwyn" (or similar) to find Elena Marchetti's message about the vendor switch. Expected findings: Elena's April 8 message in C002 (#loan-processing) explaining she had to switch the appraisal on LN-2026-00596 (9701 Selwyn Ave) from DeLuca to Carolina Valuation mid-process because DeLuca could not meet the timeline.
OE 17: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "VA" or "$525" or "$475" or "LN-2026-00241" or "Freedom Dr" (or similar) to find Sofia Reyes's message about the VA appraisal overbilling. Expected findings: Sofia's April 13 message in C002 (#loan-processing) flagging that Carolina Valuation billed $525 for the Moss VA file (LN-2026-00241) but the VA appraisal rate should be $475 -- they billed the conventional rate by mistake.
OE 18: Search for the loan on LN-2026-00241 using mortgage_los_search_loans (or mortgage_los_get_loan) with query "LN-2026-00241" (or similar) to confirm the loan type. Expected findings: LN-2026-00241 is a VA loan ($421,000), assigned to Derek Moss, closed 2026-04-23, through Freedom Mortgage. The VA loan type confirms the appraisal should be billed at $475, not $525.
OE 19: Search Stripe transfers using stripe_list_transfers (or similar) to find commission transfers to Marcus Webb's departed account. Expected findings: 5 transfers totaling $7,655 to acct_scenario_644_departed (Marcus Webb Consulting), including tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA for $2,100 on LN-2026-00608 with metadata "auto_generated: true". The connected account metadata shows "departed: 2026-03-15".
OE 20: Search for loan LN-2026-00608 using mortgage_los_search_loans (or mortgage_los_get_loan) with query "LN-2026-00608" (or similar) to verify current LO assignment. Expected findings: LN-2026-00608 is a $231,000 conventional loan through Homepoint Financial, closed 2026-04-03, assigned to James Thornton (los_staff_ef0febeeff53) -- NOT Marcus Webb. This confirms the commission was routed to the wrong person.
OE 21: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Webb" or "commission" or "Thornton" (or similar) to find Hana's email to Grace about the Webb commission leak. Expected findings: Email thread between hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Hana flags the $2,100 transfer on LN-2026-00608 as the fifth transfer to Webb's account ($7,655 total). Grace replies that the file was reassigned on March 16, instructs Hana to loop in Raj, and says to hold all April commission payouts. Grace CCs raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 22: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Webb" or "pipeline reassignment" or "LN-2026-00608" or "Thornton" (or similar) to find Grace's pipeline reassignment announcement. Expected findings: Grace's March 16 message in C002 (#loan-processing) listing the reassignments: LN-2026-00608 to James Thornton, LN-2026-00525 to Amy Chen, LN-2026-00527 to Derek Moss.
OE 23: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find Grace's pipeline reassignment note. Expected findings: CRM engagement crm_engagement_scenario_comm_01 titled "Webb pipeline reassignment complete" confirming LOS assignments updated, and noting that Stripe commission routing and QuickBooks payee records still need to be updated. This confirms the Stripe routing was flagged as needing a fix but was never completed.
OE 24: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "close-out" or "AP" or "April" or "Victor" (or similar) to find Victor's April 27 follow-up email to Hana. Expected findings: Victor asks for status on the March comp reconciliation, notes the two past-due appraisal batch bills in AP, and mentions the Webb commission overpayment needs to be reversed or reclassified for the P&L. Robert is asking for numbers tomorrow (April 28).
OE 25: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (CC victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) summarizing the full reconciliation findings across all three scenarios: the lender comp shortfalls with root causes and correct amounts for each loan, the vendor billing errors on the two appraisal batch invoices, and the ongoing Webb commission routing issue with the total misdirected amount.
OE 26: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com asking about the Pennymac comp rate change and why finance was not notified, and requesting confirmation on the AmeriHome split payment so the $1,254 can be moved from escrow trust to operating.
OE 27: Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com about the Stripe commission routing issue on LN-2026-00608 and requesting that all reassigned Webb loans have their Stripe customer metadata updated to reflect the current LO assignments.
OE 28: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in a Slack channel (C002 #loan-processing or similar) notifying the team about the vendor billing corrections needed -- specifically that the DeLuca batch bill includes a charge for LN-2026-00596 that should be billed by Carolina Valuation, and that the Carolina Valuation bill has a $525 charge on the VA file LN-2026-00241 that should be $475.
OE 29: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) documenting the April month-end reconciliation findings, including the three lender comp discrepancies with root causes and amounts, the two vendor billing errors, and the Webb commission routing issue with total exposure of $7,655.
OE 30: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 2 lookup (los_loan_cceffbf41de5 for LN-2026-00210), documenting the broker comp discrepancy -- Pennymac paid at 0.875% ($3,806.25) instead of the booked 1.25% ($5,437.50) due to the January 15 comp rate change, creating a $1,631.25 shortfall.
OE 31: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 3 lookup (los_loan_14035be9aa05 for LN-2026-00199), documenting the broker comp discrepancy -- Caliber paid at 1.0% ($3,820.00) instead of the booked 1.15% ($4,393.00) due to Grace's exception pricing approval, creating a $573.00 shortfall.
OE 32: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 4 lookup (los_loan_cb25a3f351f1 for LN-2026-00471), documenting the broker comp issue -- AmeriHome paid the correct $2,904.00 but $1,254.00 was misrouted to the escrow trust account instead of operating. Needs internal transfer to resolve.
OE 33: Add a loan activity note (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using the internal loan_id obtained from the OE 20 lookup for LN-2026-00608, documenting the commission routing error -- $2,100 commission was auto-transferred to Marcus Webb's departed account instead of James Thornton. Part of $7,655 total misdirected since Webb's March 15 departure.

2 The email to Grace (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) identifies that Pennymac reduced conventional broker compensation from 1.25% to 0.875% for loans locked on or after January 15 as the root cause of the Allen/Pennymac shortfall.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email body to Grace explicitly states the actual comp was paid at 0.875% ($3,806.25), referencing the Pennymac comp rate change from 1.25% to 0.875% effective January 15.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly states: 'Pennymac changed comp from 1.25% to 0.875% effective 1/15/2026.' Both the rate change and the January 15 cutoff date are present.
✓Run #4The agent's activity log [146] and main email [170] document that Pennymac changed conventional broker compensation from 1.25% to 0.875% effective 1/15/2026, and that Allen's loan was locked on 1/20 (after the cutoff). This root cause is included in the email to Grace.
✓Run #5The email content explicitly states: 'Pennymac changed their conventional comp schedule from 1.25% to 0.875% for locks after Jan 15, 2026.' Both the rate change (1.25% → 0.875%) and the January 15 cutoff date are clearly identified.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states: 'Pennymac updated their conventional comp schedule effective 1/15/2026, moving the rate from 1.25% to 0.875% for all locks originated after that date.' Both the rate change and the cutoff date are clearly identified.

5 The email to Grace (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) identifies the shortfall on the Allen/Pennymac loan as approximately $1,631 (the difference between the $5,437.50 booked and the $3,806.25 actual).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The email body states: 'previously projected $5,437.50; shortfall $1,631.25,' matching the required shortfall amount.

✓Run #6The email states 'Booked (incorrect): $435,000 × 1.25% = $5,437.50' and 'Revenue adjustment of -$1,631.25 needed in QB', correctly identifying the shortfall of $1,631.25.
6 The email to Grace (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) includes the root cause of the Cook/Caliber shortfall: Grace approved exception pricing at 1.0% instead of the standard 1.15% for the Cook file as a one-time deal to retain the borrower.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email identifies the Cook/Caliber root cause as a credit-score LLPA (Cook's score 692 below Caliber's 700 threshold), not as Grace's exception pricing approval at 1.0% instead of the standard 1.15%. The agent never found the CRM engagement note documenting Grace's exception pricing decision, and therefore reported an incorrect root cause.
✗Run #2The agent incorrectly attributed the Cook/Caliber shortfall to an LLPA triggered by the borrower's credit score (692 < 700 minimum), not to Grace's own exception pricing approval at 1.0% vs. 1.15%. The correct root cause (exception pricing) never appears in the trajectory.
✗Run #3The email attributes the Cook/Caliber shortfall to the borrower's credit score being 692 (below Caliber's 700 minimum) and Caliber applying exception pricing. It does not identify Grace as having approved a one-time exception pricing at 1.0% vs the standard 1.15% to retain the borrower — which is the actual root cause per the criteria.
✗Run #4The agent identified the wrong root cause for Cook/Caliber — it attributed the shortfall to a borrower credit score below 700 threshold, not to Grace's own exception pricing approval at 1.0% vs. 1.15%. The agent never found the CRM engagement note documenting Grace's approval, so this correct root cause does not appear in the email.
✗Run #5The email identifies the Cook/Caliber shortfall root cause as a credit score LLPA (borrower score 692 below Caliber's 700 threshold), not as Grace's exception pricing approval at 1.0% instead of the standard 1.15%. The agent did not discover the correct root cause from the CRM engagement note showing Grace's one-time exception pricing approval.
✗Run #6The email identifies the Cook/Caliber root cause as a borrower credit score of 692 being below Caliber's 700 minimum (LLPA adjustment), not as Grace's exception pricing approval at 1.0% vs. the standard 1.15%. The agent did not find or reference the CRM engagement note showing Grace approved exception pricing at 1.0%.

8 The email to Grace (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) identifies that the Flores/AmeriHome comp of $2,904 was paid in full, but $1,254 was misrouted to the escrow trust account instead of the operating account due to a system error by AmeriHome.

✓Run #6The email correctly explains that 'AmeriHome confirmed total comp at 1.1% = $2,904.00, but their system split the payment into two wires' with one misrouted to the escrow trust account, meaning the full comp was paid but routing was the issue.

✓Run #5The email states 'five commission transfers totaling $7,655 were routed to his Stripe connected account.' The individual amounts ($1,875 + $2,100 + $1,450 + $1,250 + $980 = $7,655) are also listed, confirmed by the Stripe transfer data.
✓Run #6The email lists all five transfers and concludes 'TOTAL: $7,655.00', clearly documenting the total amount misdirected to Webb's account.
13 A message was posted (via conversations_add_message) to a Slack channel, or the email to Grace (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) mentions, that the DeLuca batch bill (DL-4107) includes a charge for LN-2026-00596 (9701 Selwyn Ave or similar) that should be billed by Carolina Valuation instead of DeLuca, because the appraisal vendor was switched mid-process.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1The criteria require identifying that the DeLuca bill (DL-4107) includes a charge for LN-2026-00596 (9701 Selwyn Ave) that should be billed by Carolina Valuation due to a vendor switch. While the DeLuca bill does contain LN-2026-00596 as a $475 line item, neither the email to Grace nor any Slack message identifies this as the vendor-switch disputed item. The email to Grace instead flags LN-2026-00244 in the Carolina Valuation bill as the vendor-change issue, mixing up the disputed items between the two bills.
✓Run #2The Slack message and LOS activity notes reference the DeLuca bill (DL-4107) including a charge for LN-2026-00596 / 9701 Selwyn Ave that should have been billed by Carolina Valuation due to the mid-process vendor switch. This is also reflected in the corrected bill.
✗Run #3The email mentions LN-2026-00596 (James Walker) under DeLuca DL-4107 but only notes it is 'still assigned to Marcus Webb in LOS — needs reassignment.' It does not identify that the appraisal vendor was switched mid-process and the charge should instead be billed by Carolina Valuation. The vendor-switch root cause is missing.
✗Run #4The agent confused the two vendor invoices. It placed the vendor-switch mid-process issue on CVS-8831 (Carolina Valuation) with loan LN-2026-00210, rather than correctly identifying it on DL-4107 (DeLuca) for LN-2026-00596/9701 Selwyn Ave. No Slack message or email contains the correct finding.
✗Run #5The email and records incorrectly identify the vendor-switch problem as the Cook property (LN-2026-00199) rather than LN-2026-00596 (9701 Selwyn Ave). The agent actually kept LN-2026-00596 in the corrected DeLuca bill while removing LN-2026-00199. Neither the email to Grace nor any Slack message correctly identifies LN-2026-00596/9701 Selwyn Ave as the line item that should be billed by Carolina Valuation instead of DeLuca.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states 'Includes a $475 charge for 9701 Selwyn Ave (LN-2026-00596). However, this appraisal was switched mid-process from DeLuca to Carolina Valuation because DeLuca could not get an appraiser out before the rate lock window.' Both the loan/property and the vendor switch are clearly identified.
14 The Slack message (via conversations_add_message) or the email to Grace (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) mentions that the Carolina Valuation bill (CVS-8831) includes a $525 charge for a VA loan (LN-2026-00241) that should be $475, because Carolina Valuation billed the conventional rate instead of the VA rate.
outcome
 ✗—✓✗✗✓ 40%

The VA loan/wrong rate discrepancy was not correctly identified or communicated.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3The email states 'LN-2026-00241 (Timothy Edwards VA) charged $525 vs. $475 VA schedule — $50 overcharge to dispute,' identifying the $525 vs. $475 discrepancy for the VA loan.
✗Run #4The agent confused the invoices — it placed the $525 vs. $475-$500 fee discrepancy on DL-4107 (DeLuca, LN-2026-00463) rather than on CVS-8831 (Carolina Valuation, VA loan LN-2026-00241). The correct finding about the VA rate ($475 vs $525) on LN-2026-00241 does not appear in any email or Slack message.
✗Run #5The email mentions LN-2026-00241 and the $525 charge, but states the corrected VA fee is $450, not $475 as required by the criteria. The agent used the wrong corrected rate ($450 instead of $475), so the specific $525 vs $475 discrepancy is not correctly identified.
✓Run #6The email states 'Line item for 8625 Freedom Dr (LN-2026-00241) billed at $525 (conventional rate). This is a VA loan... VA appraisal rate is $475. Carolina Valuation billed the conventional rate by mistake.' The $525 vs $475 discrepancy and VA loan classification are clearly identified.
